exact_phrase_followup_searches.interpretation: No isolated supplement-form human oral PK match in top results; hits are vitamin-A-modified micelles, food bioaccessibility, or GI processing context.
Human hits are multivitamin/liposomal formulation or disease-context studies, not isolated vitamin A superiority versus standard retinyl esters.
Supports treating retinyl acetate as same preformed retinyl-ester tier as palmitate; no reason to score a full tier lower.
Mixed carotenoids have human supplementation literature, but not direct top-tier vitamin A absorption/RAE superiority evidence.
Alpha-carotene is a provitamin A carotenoid with lower RAE potency than beta-carotene; use conservative vitamin-A-activity score.
Beta-cryptoxanthin is provitamin A-active, but RAE potency remains below beta-carotene; use conservative vitamin-A-activity score.
